terminologies.meaning: algorithm for first-order gradient-based optimization of stochastic objective functions

figures.description: Logistic regression training negative log likelihood on MNIST images and IMDB movie reviews with 10,000 bag-of-words (BoW) feature vectors.

Training of multilayer neural networks on MNIST images. (a) Neural networks using dropout stochastic regularization. (b) Neural networks with deterministic cost function. We compare with the sum-of-functions (SFO) optimizer (Sohl-Dickstein et al., 2014).
Convolutional neural networks training cost. (left) Training cost for the first three epochs. (right) Training cost over 45 epochs. CIFAR-10 with c64-c64-c128-1000 architecture.

Effect of bias-correction terms (red line) versus no bias correction terms (green line) after 10 epochs (left) and 100 epochs (right) on the loss (y-axes) when learning a Variational AutoEncoder (VAE) (Kingma & Welling, 2013), for different settings of stepsize α (x-axes) and hyperparameters β₁ and β₂.

algorithms.process: See section 2 for details; pseudo‑code includes initialization of moment vectors, bias‑corrected estimates, and parameter update \( \theta_t \leftarrow \theta_{t-1} - \alpha \frac{\hat{m}_t}{\sqrt{\hat{v}_t}+\epsilon} \).

algorithms.goal: variant of Adam based on the infinity norm
algorithms.process: See section 7.1 for details; uses exponentially weighted infinity norm \(u_t = \max(\beta_2 \cdot u_{t-1}, |g_t|)\) and updates parameters with \( \theta_t \leftarrow \theta_{t-1} - \frac{\alpha}{1-\beta_1^t}\frac{m_t}{u_t} \).

weakly_supported.reason: The extraction provides a ratio definition (\hat{m}_t / \sqrt{\hat{v}_t}) but the paper uses the term informally and does not give a formal definition, making the support weak.
The extracted description is a high‑level summary and omits several important details (e.g., bias‑correction formulas, default hyper‑parameters, handling of \epsilon), so the support is limited.

ambiguous.reason: It is unclear whether the authors intend SNR as a strict statistical term or simply as a descriptive name for the ratio \hat{m}_t / \sqrt{\hat{v}_t}.
The goal "stochastic optimization" is generic, and the process description could be interpreted in multiple ways without the exact pseudo‑code.

missing: Terminology: bias‑correction term, exponential moving average, first moment (m_t), second moment (v_t), hyper‑parameters β₁, β₂, ε, learning rate α, decay schedule α_t = α·√t, RMSProp, AdaGrad, AdaDelta, vSGD, natural gradient, SFO, temporal averaging, Polyak‑Ruppert averaging.

Key theoretical results: Theorem 4.1 (regret bound), Corollary 4.2 (average regret), Lemma 10.1‑10.5, definitions of convexity, regret formulation, proof sketch, assumptions on bounded gradients and parameter distances.

Algorithmic details: exact bias‑corrected estimates \hat{m}_t = m_t/(1-β₁^t), \hat{v}_t = v_t/(1-β₂^t), default hyper‑parameter values (α=0.001, β₁=0.9, β₂=0.999, ε=10⁻⁸), step‑size annealing discussion.

Experimental summaries: quantitative performance results for logistic regression, multilayer neural networks, CNNs, VAE bias‑correction study, comparison tables/plots (e.g., training loss curves, speed comparisons with SFO, SGD, RMSProp, AdaGrad).
Extension sections: detailed derivation of AdaMax (including equations (6)‑(12)), discussion of infinity norm, bound |Δ_t| ≤ α, temporal averaging formula and bias correction.
Conclusion and future work statements not captured.
Acknowledgments and funding information beyond the brief mention.
Reference list entries (full citations) not enumerated in extraction.
Any tables present in the original paper (if any) – none were extracted.

terminologies.meaning: estimates of the 1st moment (the mean) and the 2nd raw moment (the uncentered variance) of the gradient
biased first moment estimate of the gradient
biased second raw moment estimate (elementwise square) of the gradient
a variant of Adam based on the infinity norm
exponential moving average over the parameters, \bar{\theta}_t = \beta_2 \cdot \bar{\theta}_{t-1} + (1-\beta_2) \theta_t
exponential decay rates for the moment estimates
small constant added for numerical stability

figures.description: Logistic regression training negative log likelihood on MNIST images and IMDB movie reviews with 10,000 bag-of-words (BoW) feature vectors.
Training of multilayer neural networks on MNIST images, comparing Adam with SFO, dropout, and deterministic cost.
Convolutional neural networks training cost on CIFAR-10 with c64‑c64‑c128‑1000 architecture, showing cost over epochs.
Effect of bias‑correction terms versus no bias correction on the loss when learning a Variational AutoEncoder (VAE) for different stepsize \alpha and hyperparameters \beta_1, \beta_2.

algorithms.process: initializes m_0 and v_0 to zero, iteratively computes gradients g_t, updates biased first moment m_t = \beta_1 \cdot m_{t-1} + (1-\beta_1) \cdot g_t, updates biased second moment v_t = \beta_2 \cdot v_{t-1} + (1-\beta_2) \cdot g_t^2, computes bias‑corrected estimates \hat{m}_t = m_t/(1-\beta_1^t) and \hat{v}_t = v_t/(1-\beta_2^t), and updates parameters \theta_t = \theta_{t-1} - \alpha \cdot \hat{m}_t/(\sqrt{\hat{v}_t} + \epsilon) until convergence.

initializes m_0 to zero and u_0 to zero, iteratively computes gradients g_t, updates exponentially weighted infinity norm u_t = max(\beta_2 \cdot u_{t-1}, |g_t|), updates biased first moment m_t = \beta_1 \cdot m_{t-1} + (1-\beta_1) \cdot g_t, and updates parameters \theta_t = \theta_{t-1} - (\alpha/(1-\beta_1^t)) \cdot m_t / u_t until convergence.

terminologies.meaning: variant of Adam based on the infinity norm
sum of differences between online predictions f_t(θ_t) and the best fixed point parameter f_t(θ*) over all steps
the uncentered variance (raw second moment) of the gradient
ratio of the bias‑corrected first moment estimate \(\hat m_t\) to the square root of the bias‑corrected second moment estimate \(\sqrt{\hat v_t}\)
